title: Blender Harness Gate Status
description: Single source of truth for a candidate's gate outcome, written by check-gate-status.mjs into <candidate-dir>/gate-status.json. Human-facing status docs must be generated from this file, not hand-mirrored (see issue #131 'status single source'). This file records that reviews were aggregated correctly; it never certifies visual quality itself — visual backing lives only in the referenced reviews/*.json files.

candidate_id.description: Directory-safe identifier of the candidate this status describes, e.g. 'gate-d-reduced-animation-candidate-v01'. Must match the candidate directory name.

gate.description: Which gate in the #130 Phase/Gate constitution this status is for, e.g. 'Gate D'. Free text on purpose: gate names are defined by the production constitution doc, not by this schema.

status.description: Aggregate gate outcome. The *_rejected values name which layer rejected the candidate (asset/rig/animation/pass/visual) so failure routes back to the right owner instead of a generic 'rejected'. 'route_proof_only' and 'proxy_blocking_only' mark candidates that intentionally used a disclosed proxy/blocking pass and are not eligible for downstream promotion regardless of review outcome. 'runtime_smoke_passed' only proves the WeChat runtime path loads a candidate; per issue #131 it must never be treated as, or auto-upgraded to, 'production_accepted'. 'accepted' is a generic terminal accept for gates without a more specific production status.

downstream_allowed.description: Physical unlock flag consumed by downstream build/package/runtime scripts. Must be false whenever status is not 'accepted' or 'production_accepted' (see checker rule: rejected-family or non-accepted status with downstream_allowed=true is a contract violation, checker exits 2).

required_reviews.description: Role names (e.g. 'model', 'rig', 'animation', 'pass', 'fresh-visual', 'runtime') that must each have a passing entry in reviews[] for this gate to aggregate as accepted. Any required role missing from reviews[] forces aggregate rejection.

role.description: Reviewer role name, matching one entry in required_reviews (or an optional extra role), e.g. 'model', 'rig', 'animation', 'pass', 'fresh-visual', 'runtime'.

file.description: Path (relative to the candidate directory, or repo-relative) to the reviews/<role>-review.json file this entry summarizes. Must conform to review.schema.json.

reviews.description: Pointers to the individual review.json files this gate status was aggregated from. check-gate-status.mjs reads each file's verdict to compute status; this array is the audit trail of that computation.

forbidden_next_outputs.description: Relative paths or globs (e.g. 'full-463.mp4', 'wechat-runtime-qr.png') that must not exist in the candidate directory unless status is 'accepted' or 'production_accepted'. Presence of any listed path on a non-accepted candidate is a downstream-bypass violation and fails the checker.

notes.description: Free-text human context: why this status was reached, what the next gate/repair step is, or any disclosure not captured by the structured fields above. Optional.
